d5e1_a.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_a.text: Day 5! Your final day. Everything you have built comes down to this. A health inspector arrives unannounced this morning. How prepared are you?

d5e1_b.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_b.text: Good morning. We received a complaint about your establishment. I will need to do a full inspection right now. Are you ready?

choices.text: Welcome them confidently — your shop is always inspection ready
Ask for a few minutes to tidy up quickly
Cooperate fully and answer all questions honestly
Ask to see their credentials and complaint details first

d5e1_c_ready.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_c_ready.text: Impressive. Everything is in order. Your standards are well above average. Consider this a passed inspection.

d5e1_c_stall.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_c_stall.text: A few minutes? That is highly irregular. I am noting this hesitation in my report.

d5e1_c_cooperate.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_c_cooperate.text: Thank you for your transparency. Everything checks out. You run a clean and honest operation here.

d5e1_c_verify.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_c_verify.text: Of course. Here are my credentials. The complaint was about food storage. Shall we proceed?

d5e1_result_ready.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_ready.text: Maintaining standards every day not just before inspections is what separates great businesses from average ones. Well done.

d5e1_result_stall.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_stall.text: If you need time to tidy up for an inspection your daily standards need work. Compliance should be a habit not a reaction.

d5e1_result_cooperate.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_cooperate.text: Transparency with regulators builds institutional trust. Honesty during inspections protects you far more than hiding problems.

d5e1_result_verify.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_verify.text: Knowing your rights and asking for credentials is professional and smart. Always verify authority before granting access.

d5e2_a.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_a.text: Midday crisis! A social media post is going viral claiming your shop overcharged a customer. The post is spreading fast and your phone will not stop ringing. What do you do first?

d5e2_b.speaker: You
d5e2_b.text: This is a full blown PR crisis. Every second I wait makes it worse. I need to act now...

choices.text: Release a public statement immediately addressing the issue
Investigate internally before saying anything publicly
Reach out directly to the customer who posted
Have a lawyer send a cease and desist letter

d5e2_c_statement.speaker: Follower Online
d5e2_c_statement.text: They addressed it immediately and took responsibility. That is actually really mature. Respect.

d5e2_c_investigate.speaker: Follower Online
d5e2_c_investigate.text: It has been hours and they have not said anything. The silence is making them look guilty.

d5e2_c_direct.speaker: Customer Who Posted
d5e2_c_direct.text: Wow they actually messaged me personally. That took guts. I appreciate that more than a generic post.

d5e2_c_legal.speaker: Follower Online
d5e2_c_legal.text: They sent a cease and desist to a customer?! That is insane. I am never going there. Sharing this everywhere.

d5e2_result_statement.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_statement.text: Swift transparent communication during a crisis preserves trust. Silence lets others control the narrative about your business.

d5e2_result_investigate.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_investigate.text: Internal investigation is important but so is acknowledging the issue publicly while you look into it. Both can happen simultaneously.

d5e2_result_direct.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_direct.text: Direct personal outreach during a crisis shows humanity. People remember how you made them feel far longer than what you said.

d5e2_result_legal.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_legal.text: Legal threats against customers almost always backfire catastrophically. Reserve legal action for extreme cases not PR problems.

d5e3_a.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_a.text: Final event! End of Day 5. You have come so far. A long time customer approaches you with a proposal to become a business partner and co-invest in expanding the shop. This is your biggest decision yet.

d5e3_b.speaker: Long Time Customer
d5e3_b.text: I have watched you grow this business with my own eyes. I want to invest and help you expand. I am talking a second location. What do you say?

choices.text: Accept enthusiastically — this is the opportunity you have been waiting for
Ask for time to review a formal proposal first
Propose a smaller pilot expansion before fully committing
Decline and focus on strengthening what you already have

d5e3_c_accept.speaker: Long Time Customer
d5e3_c_accept.text: Fantastic! This is going to be incredible. Let us change the game together. The city will know your name!

d5e3_c_review.speaker: Long Time Customer
d5e3_c_review.text: Of course! Smart thinking. I will have my team prepare a full proposal by end of week. Looking forward to it.

d5e3_c_pilot.speaker: Long Time Customer
d5e3_c_pilot.text: A pilot? I like that thinking. Test before you scale. You have learned a lot this week have you not?

d5e3_c_decline.speaker: Long Time Customer
d5e3_c_decline.text: I respect that. Knowing when not to expand is just as important as knowing when to grow. The offer stands whenever you are ready.

d5e3_result_accept.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_accept.text: Exciting times ahead! But always get everything in writing before celebrating. A handshake deal is not a business deal.

d5e3_result_review.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_review.text: Never let excitement override due diligence. Reviewing proposals carefully protects you from partnerships that look good but lack substance.

d5e3_result_pilot.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_pilot.text: Testing before scaling is one of the most valuable lessons in business. You have truly grown this week. This is the right instinct.

d5e3_result_decline.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_decline.text: Sustainable growth beats reckless expansion every time. Consolidating your foundation before scaling is wisdom not fear.
